title: Yahir's Al Forthan Scholarship Application
html:
Yahir's full Al Forthan Scholarship essay set — written in January 2023 for the Volunteers of America Oregon scholarship that recognizes children of alcoholics. Five prompts covering teen drug use solutions, race/class incarceration disparities, his volunteer work, financial barriers, and a long personal essay on growing up with his alcoholic father.

TL;DR

  • Most personally revealing scholarship essay of the college application arc: names his father's addiction, the household abuse, his role as protector, and the path toward forgiveness.
  • Cites josephine county food bank, mecha organization (President), FBLA (Treasurer), young entrepreneurs business week (Nick Fisher Award), gtanos (family restaurant), and mini pet mart as the activities table.
  • Frames late-2019 parental separation and his summer pet store job as the inflection where he became financial backstop for yahir mom.
  • Positions al forthan as a network of others living with addiction — Yahir wants to be "the example, like Al" for future mentees.
  • Voice: heavily polished application register; the source draft underneath is the same emotional spine that erupts raw in the 2023 01 25 Thoughts on 1242023 journal entries.

Key quote

"Drinking from dawn to dusk damaged my family. I became the protector of my household."

Why it matters in the graph

This is the canonical, full-length version of the yahir dad addiction arc. Many later 2023 docs (2023 02 28 Ford Family Foundation DRAFT Emilio Gonzalez, 2023 03 01 ford finals, 2023 11 07 Redwood Foundation Essays, 2023 10 04 Al Forthan Update) reference back to fragments of this essay — they're polished derivatives. Land here when grepping for the full origin story.
